    Home › Aircraft › Ilyushin Il-96

    Ilyushin Il-96

    Ilyushin · Widebody · In limited service

    The four-engine flagship of Russian aviation.

    Length63.94 m
    Wingspan60.11 m
    Range10,000 km
    MTOW270 t
    Typical seats300
    Built33
    Our numbers

    Derived metrics

    Computed by aircraft.fyi from the core specification — see how we calculate these.

    Seats per metre#13 of 30
    4.69 seats/m typical seats ÷ fuselage length
    Range per tonne#29 of 40
    37.0 km/t maximum range ÷ MTOW in tonnes
    Span-to-length ratio#15 of 43
    0.94 wingspan ÷ fuselage length
    Weight per metre#18 of 43
    4.22 t/m MTOW ÷ fuselage length
    Range in hours#15 of 40
    11.1 h maximum range ÷ cruise speed
    Weight per seat#9 of 30
    900 kg MTOW ÷ typical seats
    Sense of scale

    How big is it, really?

    The Ilyushin Il-96's wingspan, against things you already know the size of.

    Ilyushin Il-9660.11 m
    Football pitch (width)68 m
    Blue whale25 m
    New Routemaster bus11.2 m
    You (probably)1.75 m

    Specification

    The numbers

    Length (Il-96-400)63.94 m
    Wingspan60.11 m
    Height15.70 m
    MTOW (-400)270,000 kg
    Range (-300)≈10,000 km
    Typical seats≈300
    Engines4 × Aviadvigatel PS-90A
    First flight28 September 1988
    Built≈33

    Compare it

    Last verified: 2026-07-14 · Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    The story

    What makes it different

    The Il-96 is a four-engine, wingletted widebody that first flew in 1988. It arrived just as the airliner market was opening to worldwide competition, and only around 33 were completed.

    Its most famous role is as Russia's presidential aircraft — the Il-96-300PU — with a small fleet operated by the Special Flight Detachment for state transport. Cubana flew it as the last airline operator.

    Production has recently restarted with the stretched Il-96-400M, which at 63.94 metres is one of the longest aircraft ever built. Whether it returns to regular airline service is the open question for the type.

    Variants

    The Ilyushin Il-96 line

    Il-96-300
    The 55.35 m original — the presidential fleet.
    Il-96-400T / -400M
    The 63.94 m stretch — freighter and relaunched passenger version.
    Operators

    Who flies the Ilyushin Il-96

    The top 2 operators by fleet size, of ≈3 worldwide — the rest are aggregated below. Counts marked ≈ are approximate, verified 2026-07-14.

    RURossiya Special Flight DetachmentThe presidential and state fleet — including the -300PU command aircraft.≈8
    CUCubana de AviaciónThe last airline operator — availability varies.≈3
    Other operatorsA handful of Russian state and cargo aircraft.≈2

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
